tasks.system: You are writing internal Slack/Discord alert message templates. Output in markdown. Concise, no marketing language, designed to fire from webhooks.
tasks.prompt:
Write Slack/Discord alert message templates for every internal notification the platform fires. Format: labeled section per trigger + the message body with {{placeholders}}. Use Slack Block Kit-style markdown (bold + emoji-prefix) where helpful.

Triggers:
- New subscription activated → #mbt-revenue channel
- Subscription past due → #mbt-revenue (urgent tag)
- Subscription cancelled → #mbt-revenue
- Order paid → #mbt-orders
- Order shipped → #mbt-orders
- Quote submitted (private event) → #inn-ops (Tracy urgent)
- New artist application to Records → #records
- New broadcast scheduled → #broadcast
- Broadcast went live → #broadcast (all hands)
- Broadcast ended → #broadcast
- Dead-letter event appeared → #ops (engineer on-call)
- Tenant provisioned → #mbt-ops
- Cron job failed (any) → #ops
- New Sanity content published (Tracy hits Publish) → #content
- Wizard draft saved → #content (Tracy)
- New FAQ submitted for review → #content
- Immich storage > 80% → #ops
- Stripe dispute opened → #mbt-revenue urgent
- Mac mini broadcast agent offline for >5min → #ops urgent

For each: channel, urgency level, message template with placeholders, suggested action buttons (if any).

Markdown. ~2000 words.

tasks.system: You are writing social media launch posts announcing the MBT platform across every brand's social account. Output in markdown with labeled platform sections. No preamble.
tasks.prompt:
Write social media launch posts for each brand announcing the MBT platform is live (or each brand's new subscription/product offering). Per-brand + per-platform. Platforms: Instagram feed, Instagram Story (3 frames), TikTok caption, X/Twitter, Facebook. 5-8 posts per brand.

Brands: Inn, Magazine, Touring, Records, Radio, Chase Pierson Photography, Tuthill Design, Studio C.

For each post: platform, post type, copy (character-limited per platform), hashtags (3 max), CTA, any image/video direction brackets.

Voice rules per brand from docs/voice/. Never 'exciting announcement', 'thrilled to launch', emojis unless platform demands, 'Don't miss out!'.

Markdown. ~4000 words.

tasks.system: You are writing Sanity-ready FAQ seed data. Output in markdown with a structured format per entry that can be easily parsed for Sanity import. No preamble.
tasks.prompt:
Write the 50 most-important FAQ entries that should exist on launch day across all brands. Format each as:

```
### [Brand] — [Category] — [Question]
_orderRank: [number]_
[Answer body in plain prose]

---
```

Pick 6-8 per brand covering the categories from the per-brand FAQs in docs/customer-faqs/*.md. Prioritize questions real customers actually ask:

Inn: Booking, rooms, dining, Blues Room, Natchez, accessibility
Tuthill: Pricing, turnaround, what's included, cancellation, markets
Records: Masters, exclusivity, distribution, royalties, application
Radio: Reach, packages, reporting, exclusivity
Touring: Booking flow, venues, transportation, non-exclusivity
DSD: Free vs paid, listing edits, reviews, SEO
Inn + Magazine: cross-referenced (what's the Magazine about if I'm staying at the Inn?)

Answers: 2-4 sentences each. Voice matches the brand. No marketing fluff.

Markdown. ~4000 words.

tasks.system: You are writing a real press release announcing the MBT platform is operational. Output in markdown, AP-style. Chase's voice.
tasks.prompt:
Write the press release announcing the MBT platform is live. Target publications: Mississippi Business Journal, Hudson Valley One, local Natchez press, music-industry trades where relevant.

AP-style format. Headline (8-12 words), dateline, lede (5 W's), 3-4 body paragraphs, about boilerplate, media contact section.

Key points: MBT (Measurably Better Things LLC) operates a platform across music-hospitality-media properties; three equal partners; Mississippi corridor + Hudson Valley node; 7 modules; what it does for small businesses (Tuthill realtor services, DSD); first broker-tier client in pipeline (don't name Paul Green unless quoted).

Voice: Chase. NEVER 'groundbreaking', 'innovative', 'comprehensive solution'. Specific numbers where useful.

Markdown. ~1500 words.

tasks.system: You are writing a one-pager for MBT targeting angel / small-VC investors. Output in markdown. Specific, number-anchored, Chase's voice.
tasks.prompt:
Write a one-pager for MBT targeting angel investors / small-check VCs. Single page when printed. Sections:

1. Headline (10 words — what MBT is in one sentence)
2. The thesis (2-3 sentences — why now, why this, why us)
3. The three layers diagram (ASCII or described — HDI / MBT / Implementations)
4. The metrics (with placeholders Chase fills in from real data): MRR, active subscribers, tenants onboarded, bucket-hours consumed, content units published/month
5. The three concentric revenue motions (internal tooling / vendor one-off / Tier 3 recurring platform fee 15-30%)
6. The moat (Mississippi corridor + content engine + tenant-provisioning speed + the brand family)
7. The team (Chase + Tracy + Amy — one line each)
8. The ask (funding range + use of funds)
9. Contact

Markdown. ~1200 words (intentionally dense). [PLACEHOLDERS] for numbers Chase fills in.

tasks.system: You are writing a GROQ (Sanity query language) cheatsheet for engineers. Output in markdown with code blocks.
tasks.prompt:
Write a cheatsheet of the most-used GROQ queries for the MBT Sanity project. Cover the schemas we have: article, location, event, touringPage, contentTemplate, podcastEpisode, magazineIssue, showEvent, staff, partnerArtist, pressRelease, faq.

For each schema, provide 3-5 common queries:
- List all published, ordered by date
- Get single by slug
- Related queries (articles by staff member; episodes by show; FAQs by brand/category; etc.)

Also cover: drafts vs published handling (drafts.* prefix), image asset URLs + transformations, references resolution (->), filtering by reference array (in), portable text resolution.

Markdown. ~2500 words.

tasks.system: You are writing a quarterly investor update email template. Output in markdown. Specific, number-anchored, no corporate-fluff.
tasks.prompt:
Write the quarterly investor update email template (first one would be Q2 2026). Sections:

- Subject line (3 variants — something like 'MBT Q2 2026 update')
- Opening paragraph — 1 sentence headline takeaway
- Numbers table (MRR, customers, Tier 3 engagements, churn, bucket hours consumed, revenue, runway)
- Wins this quarter (3-5 bullets with specifics — no vague 'good momentum')
- Losses / misses this quarter (3-5 bullets — honest about what didn't work)
- What shipped (modules + features + brand work)
- Pipeline (named brokers / realtors / artists / partners — placeholders)
- The next quarter — top 3 priorities with owners + deadlines
- Asks — what we need from investors (intros, feedback, follow-on)
- Sign-off (Chase + Tracy + Amy)

All fields with [PLACEHOLDERS]. Voice: direct, specific, no BS. NEVER 'exciting', 'thrilled', 'tremendous momentum'.

Markdown. ~2000 words.
